_source: NASM CPT, ACE Personal Trainer Manual, Exercise Physiology textbooks, Precision Nutrition
_note: Long-tail fitness knowledge. Exercise science, program design, form cues, nutrition basics, injury prevention.

atp_cp_phosphagen.recovery: 30 sec to 3 min for full replenishment

glycolytic_anaerobic.fuel: Muscle glycogen converted to ATP without oxygen

oxidative_aerobic.fuel: Carbs and fats with oxygen; unlimited duration if pace sustainable

type_i_slow_twitch.characteristics: Fatigue-resistant, aerobic, low force, slow contraction
type_i_slow_twitch.training_response: Endurance training increases mitochondrial density and capillaries
type_i_slow_twitch.best_for: Marathon running, cycling long distance, postural muscles

type_iia_fast_twitch_oxidative.characteristics: Moderate force, moderate fatigue resistance, hybrid aerobic/anaerobic
type_iia_fast_twitch_oxidative.training_response: Adaptable; can shift toward Type I or IIx based on training

type_iix_fast_twitch_glycolytic.characteristics: High force, fast contraction, fatigue quickly, anaerobic
type_iix_fast_twitch_glycolytic.training_response: Heavy strength training, plyometrics increase size and power

progressive_overload.principle: Gradually increase stimulus to force adaptation
methods: Increase reps (8 reps → 12 reps at same weight)
Decrease rest (90 sec → 60 sec between sets)
Increase tempo (slower eccentric 4 sec instead of 2 sec)
Increase range of motion (half squat → full squat)
progressive_overload.minimum_effective_dose: Smallest stimulus that produces adaptation; avoid excessive volume (diminishing returns and injury risk)

strength_1_5_reps.adaptations: Neural efficiency, maximal force production, minimal hypertrophy

hypertrophy_6_12_reps.adaptations: Muscle growth, metabolic stress, mechanical tension
hypertrophy_6_12_reps.volume: 10-20 sets per muscle group per week

muscular_endurance_12_20_reps.adaptations: Fatigue resistance, capillary density, mitochondrial function

periodization.linear: Progress from high volume/low intensity → low volume/high intensity over 8-12 weeks; beginner-friendly
periodization.undulating: Vary intensity and volume within week; Monday strength, Wednesday hypertrophy, Friday endurance; prevents adaptation staleness
periodization.block: Focus on one quality for 3-4 weeks, then switch; accumulation (volume) → intensification (load) → realization (peaking)
periodization.deload: Every 4th week reduce volume 40-60% or intensity 10-20%; recovery week; prevents overtraining

movement_patterns.horizontal_push: Bench press, push-ups; pectorals, anterior deltoid, triceps
movement_patterns.vertical_push: Overhead press, handstand push-up; deltoids, upper chest, triceps
movement_patterns.horizontal_pull: Rows (barbell, dumbbell, cable); rhomboids, mid traps, lats, rear delts
movement_patterns.vertical_pull: Pull-ups, lat pulldowns; lats, lower traps, biceps
movement_patterns.squat: Back squat, front squat, goblet squat; quads, glutes, adductors
movement_patterns.hinge: Deadlift, RDL, kettlebell swing; hamstrings, glutes, spinal erectors
movement_patterns.lunge: Split squat, walking lunge, Bulgarian split squat; single-leg strength, stability
movement_patterns.carry: Farmer's walk, suitcase carry; core stability, grip, postural muscles

exercise_selection.compound_vs_isolation: Compound = multi-joint (squat, bench); most bang for buck; Isolation = single-joint (bicep curl, leg extension); targeted work
exercise_selection.bilateral_vs_unilateral: Bilateral = both limbs (barbell squat); more load; Unilateral = one limb (single-leg RDL); balance, address asymmetries
exercise_selection.order: Heavy compounds first (squat, deadlift, bench), then accessories, then isolation; fatigue management

squat_cues.setup: Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out 10-15°; bar on upper traps (high bar) or rear delts (low bar)
squat_cues.descent: Break at hips and knees simultaneously; knees track over toes; chest up; neutral spine
squat_cues.depth: Hip crease below knee (parallel) for full ROM; partial if mobility limited
squat_cues.ascent: Drive through midfoot; knees out; hips and chest rise together; avoid good morning squat (hips rise first)
squat_cues.breathing: Inhale at top, brace core (Valsalva), descend, ascend, exhale at top or partway up
squat_cues.common_faults: Knees caving (valgus collapse) - cue knees out; excessive forward lean - chest up; heels lifting - ankle mobility or squat shoes

deadlift_cues.setup: Feet hip-width; bar over midfoot; shins touch bar; grip just outside legs; shoulders over bar
deadlift_cues.starting_position: Chest up; lats engaged (pull bar into shins); neutral spine; hips higher than knees but lower than shoulders
deadlift_cues.pull: Push floor away with legs; bar drags up shins; hips and shoulders rise together; finish standing tall with glutes squeezed
deadlift_cues.lowering: Hinge at hips first; bar path straight down; control descent; reset each rep
deadlift_cues.breathing: Inhale before pull, brace, lift, exhale at top or after lowering
deadlift_cues.common_faults: Rounded back - reset with chest up; bar drifts forward - lats not engaged; hips shoot up - start with hips slightly lower

bench_press_cues.setup: Feet flat on floor; arch in lower back; shoulder blades retracted and depressed; eyes under bar
bench_press_cues.unrack: Straight up, then move over chest; lockout position
bench_press_cues.descent: Elbows 45-75° from torso (not 90° flared); bar to nipple line or lower chest; forearms vertical
bench_press_cues.press: Drive feet into floor; leg drive through arch; press bar up and slightly back to start position
bench_press_cues.breathing: Inhale during descent, hold at bottom, exhale during press or at top
bench_press_cues.common_faults: Elbows flared 90° - shoulder impingement risk; bouncing off chest - no control; butt off bench - lose stability

overhead_press_cues.setup: Feet hip-width; bar on front delts; elbows slightly forward of bar; grip just outside shoulders
overhead_press_cues.press: Press bar up and slightly back once past face; squeeze glutes to prevent hyperextending lumbar spine; shrug shoulders at top
overhead_press_cues.lockout: Bar over midfoot; biceps by ears; active overhead position
overhead_press_cues.descent: Control down to front delts; elbows track forward
overhead_press_cues.breathing: Inhale at bottom, brace, press, exhale at top
overhead_press_cues.common_faults: Leaning back excessively - hyperextends spine; bar path forward - loses power; not shrugging at top - incomplete lockout

warm_up_structure.general_warm_up: 5-10 min low-intensity cardio; raise body temp, heart rate; cycling, rowing, dynamic movements
warm_up_structure.dynamic_stretching: Leg swings, arm circles, walking lunges, inchworms; 5-10 min; active ROM
warm_up_structure.specific_warm_up: Movement-specific prep; empty bar squat 10 reps, then 50% 1RM 5 reps, 70% 3 reps, working weight; grease the groove
warm_up_structure.activation: Glute bridges, band pull-aparts, dead bugs; activate underactive muscles before compound lifts

mobility_vs_flexibility.flexibility: Passive range of motion; static stretching; hold 30-60 sec post-workout
mobility_vs_flexibility.mobility: Active range of motion with control; dynamic movements; more functional than pure flexibility
mobility_vs_flexibility.priorities: Hip flexor mobility (psoas); thoracic spine rotation; ankle dorsiflexion; shoulder external rotation

lower_back_pain.causes: Poor squat/deadlift form; weak core; anterior pelvic tilt; tight hip flexors
lower_back_pain.prevention: Master hip hinge; strengthen core (planks, dead bugs, bird dogs); stretch hip flexors; avoid hyperextension
lower_back_pain.management: If acute, rest 2-3 days; ice; light movement; avoid loaded flexion; seek professional if persists >1 week

shoulder_impingement.causes: Overhead pressing with poor form; internal rotation dominance; weak rotator cuff
shoulder_impingement.prevention: Face pulls, band pull-aparts; external rotation work; avoid benching with flared elbows
shoulder_impingement.management: Reduce overhead volume; increase horizontal pulling 2:1 ratio to pressing; strengthen rotator cuff

knee_pain.causes: Valgus collapse in squat; excessive running volume; quad/hamstring imbalance; poor ankle mobility
knee_pain.prevention: Knees out cue; strengthen glutes; balance quad and hamstring work; improve ankle dorsiflexion
knee_pain.management: Reduce range of motion (box squat); terminal knee extensions; hip strengthening

tennis_elbow_lateral_epicondylitis.causes: Overuse; excessive gripping; bicep curls with too much weight; poor wrist position in pressing
tennis_elbow_lateral_epicondylitis.prevention: Neutral wrist in all exercises; don't over-grip bar; wrist extensor stretches
tennis_elbow_lateral_epicondylitis.management: Reduce volume; eccentric wrist extensions; theraband flexbar; avoid aggravating movements

protein.recommendation: 0.7-1.0 g/lb bodyweight; 1.0-1.2 if lean/muscle building; 0.6-0.8 if high bodyfat
protein.timing: Distribute evenly across meals; 20-40g per meal; pre/post workout not critical if daily total met
protein.sources: Chicken, fish, lean beef, eggs, Greek yogurt, cottage cheese, whey protein, tofu, legumes

carbohydrates.recommendation: 2-4 g/lb bodyweight depending on activity; higher for athletes, lower for sedentary
carbohydrates.timing: Pre-workout (1-3 hr before); post-workout (within 2 hr) for glycogen replenishment
carbohydrates.sources: Rice, oats, potatoes, sweet potatoes, fruit, whole grains, vegetables

fats.recommendation: 0.3-0.5 g/lb bodyweight; minimum 0.3 for hormone production
fats.types: Prioritize unsaturated (avocado, nuts, olive oil, fatty fish); limit saturated; avoid trans fats
fats.sources: Avocado, nuts, nut butter, olive oil, salmon, eggs, seeds

calorie_targets.maintenance: Bodyweight (lbs) × 14-16; sedentary 14, active 16; or TDEE calculator
calorie_targets.fat_loss: Maintenance - 300-500 cal; 0.5-1% bodyweight loss per week; preserve muscle with high protein
calorie_targets.muscle_gain: Maintenance + 200-300 cal; 0.25-0.5% bodyweight gain per week; more = excessive fat gain
calorie_targets.tracking: MyFitnessPal, Cronometer; track 3-7 days to assess intake; adjust based on weekly average weight

hydration.baseline: Bodyweight (lbs) / 2 = oz per day; 180 lb = 90 oz water
hydration.activity: Add 12-16 oz per hour of exercise
hydration.indicators: Urine pale yellow; thirst rare; dark urine or persistent thirst = dehydrated

essential: Protein powder (convenience; not necessary if whole food protein adequate)
Creatine monohydrate 5g/day (strength, power, muscle; most researched supplement; safe)
beneficial: Caffeine 3-6 mg/kg (performance, focus; pre-workout 30-60 min before)
Vitamin D 2000-4000 IU if deficient (most people are; get tested)
supplements.not_necessary: BCAAs (protein already contains), glutamine (not ergogenic), testosterone boosters (ineffective unless prescription)

methods.scale_weight: Least accurate for composition; affected by water, food, time of day; track weekly average
methods.progress_photos: Front, side, back; same lighting/time/clothing; every 2-4 weeks; most practical
methods.measurements: Waist, hips, chest, arms, thighs; track every 2-4 weeks; sensitive to fat loss/muscle gain
methods.dexa_scan: Gold standard; expensive ($100-200); shows body fat %, lean mass, bone density
methods.bioelectrical_impedance: Scale/handheld; inaccurate for absolute values; useful for trends if consistent timing

fitness_testing.cardiovascular: 1-mile run, beep test, VO2 max test; track time or distance improvements
fitness_testing.strength: 1RM test or 3RM; estimate 1RM = weight × reps × 0.0333 + weight
fitness_testing.muscular_endurance: Push-up test, plank hold, bodyweight squat reps in 1 min
fitness_testing.movement_screening: FMS (Functional Movement Screen); identifies asymmetries and limitations; injury risk
